questionHtml: The proportion of banded and unbanded individuals in a population of D. plantarius can change. Suggest how such a change could occur.
markschemeHtml:
1. natural selection could favour one type over the other;
  2. greater survival of one type
    OR
    one type better camouflaged than the other type;
  3. one type more attractive during courtship/mate selection;

 

Do not accept a list. Natural selection or sexual selections alone are not adequate.

meta.title: The proportion of banded and unbanded individuals in a population of D. plantarius can change. Suggest how such a change could occur.
breadcrumbLabels: D3.2.5. Effects of dominant and recessive alleles on phenotype
